name: Suspicious CLR Logs Creation
notes:
This rule detects suspicious .NET assembly executions.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: omkar72, oscd.community, Wojciech Lesicki
SIGMA Title: Suspicious CLR Logs Creation
SIGMA Description: Detects suspicious .NET assembly executions. Could detect using Cobalt Strike's command execute-assembly.


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001830) FileCreate
when the event matches Target File Directory (custom) is any of [\AppData\Local\Microsoft\CLR or \UsageLogs]
when the event matches Target File Directory (custom) contains any of [mshta or cscript or wscript or regsvr32 or wmic or rundll32 or svchost]
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious CLR Logs Creation on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001830) FileCreate
AND when the event matches Target File Directory (custom) is any of [\AppData\Local\Microsoft\CLR or \UsageLogs]
AND when the event matches Target File Directory (custom) contains any of [mshta or cscript or wscript or regsvr32 or wmic or rundll32 or svchost]
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious CLR Logs Creation
Event description: Detects suspicious .NET assembly executions.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
